description: Build integration: add Flutter build scripts to package.json, update next.config.ts with /app/* rewrite rule, and create the app/api/flutter-auth/route.ts authentication bridge endpoint. Covers Phases 7-8.
steps: Add to package.json scripts: 'build:flutter': 'cd msm_app && flutter build web --release --dart-define=SITE_URL=$NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL --base-href /app/ --output ../public/app'
Replace cap:build:android in package.json with 'cap:build:flutter:android': 'cd msm_app && flutter build apk --release --dart-define=SITE_URL=https://msmsupermarket.in'

Update next.config.ts: add a rewrites() function that returns { source: '/app/:path*', destination: '/app/index.html' } as a fallback rewrite so Flutter's client-side routing works for all /app/* paths

Create app/api/flutter-auth/route.ts: POST endpoint that accepts { phone, otp, name? } in the body, calls verifyLatestOtp(phone, otp) from lib/otp.ts, on success upserts the user (same logic as phone-otp credentials provider in auth.ts), signs a JWT using jsonwebtoken with the NEXTAUTH_SECRET containing { id, role, name, email, phone }, returns { token, user } on success or { error } on failure
Run flutter analyze in msm_app/ to confirm Flutter app still compiles cleanly
acceptance_criteria: package.json has build:flutter and cap:build:flutter:android scripts
next.config.ts has rewrites() returning fallback for /app/:path*
app/api/flutter-auth/route.ts exists and handles POST with phone+otp verification, returns JWT
The auth bridge uses the same OTP verification logic as the existing phone-otp provider
verification: Run: export PATH=/opt/flutter/bin:$PATH && cd /projects/sandbox/revathysupermarket/msm_app && flutter analyze
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/revathysupermarket && npx tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck app/api/flutter-auth/route.ts || echo 'TypeScript check done'
findings: TypeScript type-checking fails across the entire project due to missing node_modules (npm install not run). This is a pre-existing condition unrelated to FEAT-004 changes. Flutter analyze passes cleanly. The flutter-auth route follows the exact same upsert pattern as the phone-otp credentials provider in auth.ts.
